title: Sustainable Vertical Micro-Garden for Urban Housing
overview_text: A sustainable urban planning project proposing a scalable rooftop micro-garden system optimized for high-rise residential environments.
problem_statement: Lack of space in high-density urban housing restricts access to fresh produce and disconnects residents from sustainable food sources.
objectives: Propose a cost-effective, space-efficient garden layout.
Use decision matrices to validate design alternatives.
Develop a localized bill of materials for deployment.
engineering_process: Literature review, development of multi-criteria weighted decision matrix, system design in CAD, and structural weight analysis.
technical_details: 10m² footprint; Budget: 74,200 LKR; Utilized lightweight organic substrates.
design_decisions: Selected Green Rooftop architecture for its high social and environmental impact score.
challenges: Accounting for structural weight limits on existing rooftops.
results: Delivered a fully validated, CAD-modeled design proposal within strict budget constraints.
key_learning: Learned to convert qualitative sustainability goals into quantitative decision-making parameters.
